trans-unit.source: How to generate Risk Indicators
trans-unit.target: How to generate Risk Indicators

trans-unit.source: To conduct a meaningful risk assessment with the [Risk Assessment QGIS Plugin](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_risk_assessment_plugin.html), it is essential to prepare input indicator files that represent the three main dimensions:
trans-unit.target: To conduct a meaningful risk assessment with the [Risk Assessment QGIS Plugin](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_risk_assessment_plugin.html), it is essential to prepare input indicator files that represent the three main dimensions:

trans-unit.source: **Exposure** (mandatory)
trans-unit.target: **Exposure** (mandatory)

trans-unit.source: **Vulnerability** (mandatory)
trans-unit.target: **Vulnerability** (mandatory)

trans-unit.source: **Coping Capacity** (optional, but strongly recommended)
trans-unit.target: **Coping Capacity** (optional, but strongly recommended)

trans-unit.source: This page provides detailed video examples of how to generate each of these indicators using commonly available geospatial data of Ethiopia. The goal is to support users in creating well-structured and valid inputs that align with the plugin’s requirements.
trans-unit.target: This page provides detailed video examples of how to generate each of these indicators using commonly available geospatial data of Ethiopia. The goal is to support users in creating well-structured and valid inputs that align with the plugin’s requirements.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Key Takeaways :class: tip
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Key Takeaways :class: tip

trans-unit.source: Ensure the file contains an `ADM_PCODE` column.
trans-unit.target: Ensure the file contains an `ADM_PCODE` column.

trans-unit.source: Only include indicator columns relevant for the specific dimension.
trans-unit.target: Only include indicator columns relevant for the specific dimension.

trans-unit.source: Choose unique column names that describe the content of the column briefly but efficiently. :::
trans-unit.target: Choose unique column names that describe the content of the column briefly but efficiently. :::

trans-unit.source: Exposure Indicators
trans-unit.target: Exposure Indicators

trans-unit.source: The Expousre indicator was determined by combining the [Aridity index (AI)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/icpac-geonode-ethiopia-aridity) and the [Palmer Drought Severity Index (PDSI)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/ethiopia-palmer-drought-severity-index-pdsi-2020-2024) from **HDX** to determine the drought risk of the administrative units.
trans-unit.target: The Expousre indicator was determined by combining the [Aridity index (AI)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/icpac-geonode-ethiopia-aridity) and the [Palmer Drought Severity Index (PDSI)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/ethiopia-palmer-drought-severity-index-pdsi-2020-2024) from **HDX** to determine the drought risk of the administrative units.

trans-unit.source: Aridity index (AI)
trans-unit.target: Aridity index (AI)

trans-unit.source: The raster file was opened in QGIS and the mean drought index per administrative unit was calculated using `Zonal statistics`:
trans-unit.target: The raster file was opened in QGIS and the mean drought index per administrative unit was calculated using `Zonal statistics`:

trans-unit.source: Palmer Drought Severity Index (PDSI)
trans-unit.target: Palmer Drought Severity Index (PDSI)

trans-unit.source: First, the raw data was pivoted and processed in Excel to calculate the average PDSI between 2020 and 2024:
trans-unit.target: First, the raw data was pivoted and processed in Excel to calculate the average PDSI between 2020 and 2024:

trans-unit.source: Then, the processed data was opened in QGIS and added to the exposure indicator file:
trans-unit.target: Then, the processed data was opened in QGIS and added to the exposure indicator file:

trans-unit.source: Vulnerability Indicators
trans-unit.target: Vulnerability Indicators

trans-unit.source: The Vulnerability indicator was determined by combining the [ACLED - Conflict Events](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/ethiopia-acled-conflict-data) and [Worldpop](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/worldpop-age-and-gender-structures-2015-2030-eth) datasets from **HDX**.
trans-unit.target: The Vulnerability indicator was determined by combining the [ACLED - Conflict Events](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/ethiopia-acled-conflict-data) and [Worldpop](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/worldpop-age-and-gender-structures-2015-2030-eth) datasets from **HDX**.

trans-unit.source: ACLED - Conflict Events
trans-unit.target: ACLED - Conflict Events

trans-unit.source: The dataset was processed in QGIS to estimate the population affected by conflicts in 2024 using the `Join Attributes by Location (Summary)` tool. Additionally the number of conflicts per administrative unit was calculated using the `Count Points in Polygon` tool:
trans-unit.target: The dataset was processed in QGIS to estimate the population affected by conflicts in 2024 using the `Join Attributes by Location (Summary)` tool. Additionally the number of conflicts per administrative unit was calculated using the `Count Points in Polygon` tool:

trans-unit.source: Worldpop
trans-unit.target: Worldpop

trans-unit.source: The dataset was processed in QGIS to estimate the vulnerable population (<15 years & >65 years) in 2024 using the `Raster Calculator` and the `Zonal statistics` tools:
trans-unit.target: The dataset was processed in QGIS to estimate the vulnerable population (<15 years & >65 years) in 2024 using the `Raster Calculator` and the `Zonal statistics` tools:

trans-unit.source: Coping Capacity Indicators
trans-unit.target: Coping Capacity Indicators

trans-unit.source: The Coping Capacity indicator was determined by combining the [Health Facilities](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hotosm_eth_health_facilities) and the [Education Facilities](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hotosm_eth_education_facilities) from **HDX**.
trans-unit.target: The Coping Capacity indicator was determined by combining the [Health Facilities](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hotosm_eth_health_facilities) and the [Education Facilities](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hotosm_eth_education_facilities) from **HDX**.

trans-unit.source: Health and Ecudation Facilities
trans-unit.target: Health and Ecudation Facilities

trans-unit.source: The dataset was processed in QGIS to count the number of facilities per administrative unit using the `Count Points in Polygon` tool:
trans-unit.target: The dataset was processed in QGIS to count the number of facilities per administrative unit using the `Count Points in Polygon` tool:
